# RDP entry-host pivot and crown-jewel risk band per user (T1021.001)

**Dataset**: `gx_06_rdp`

Profiles each user's RDP movement: an inner join to the `gx_06_hosts` inventory tags each target's `tier`, `windowcomp first_value` (ordered by epoch time) captures the user's initial pivot host, and a per-user rollup counts crown-jewel hits and target breadth. A nested `if()` assigns a CRITICAL / ELEVATED / LOW risk band from the crown-jewel count. Demonstrates the join-alias scope rule — the joined `tier` is read by **bare name** downstream (the alias `h` belongs only to the join predicate `h.host = dst_host`) — and using `to_epoch(ts)` to give `windowcomp` a sortable numeric key.

```sql
dataset = gx_06_rdp
| join type = inner (dataset = gx_06_hosts) as h h.host = dst_host
| alter et = to_epoch(ts), cj = if(tier = "crown-jewel", 1, 0)
| windowcomp first_value(dst_host) by user sort asc et as entry_host
| comp count_distinct(dst_host) as hosts_reached, sum(cj) as cj_hits, values(tier) as tiers, first(entry_host) as entry by user
| alter risk = if(cj_hits >= 3, "CRITICAL", cj_hits >= 1, "ELEVATED", "LOW")
| fields user, hosts_reached, cj_hits, entry, risk, tiers
| sort desc cj_hits, desc hosts_reached
```
